attacking_style: Specializes in long-range solo attacks (10-12km out) on high-mountain stages, often bridging to or emerging from breakaways rather than launching from GC groups. Thrives on steep gradients (21% ramps) and final climb finishes.
team_tactics: Operates primarily without team support in mountains; relies on tactical positioning within breakaways and exploiting GC teams' mutual marking. No evidence of sustained domestique support or leadout trains.
terrain_preference: Steep alpine climbs with sustained gradients above 10%. Excels on multi-summit stages where KOM points accumulation creates tactical opportunities. Vulnerable in crosswinds, flat sprint finishes, and technical descents where crashes occur.
vulnerability: Crash-prone in demanding stage race conditions (abandoned Paris-Nice twice via crashes). Shows hesitation in reduced sprint finishes when former climber reputation invites marking. Form inconsistency between seasons prevents reliable year-over-year performance.
key_patterns: Sits on stronger GC riders' chase efforts, then outkicks them in finale (2017 Tour x2)
Actively hunts KOM points mid-stage while GC contenders marked, building jersey leads (2017 Tour x3)
Wins from breakaways by timing late splits perfectly, not raw power dominance (2017, 2023)
Crash abandonments in stage races reveal durability/positioning issues under pressure (2019, 2020)
Form cycles dramatically: 2017 peak, 2018-19 decline, 2022-23 resurgence at age 30+
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Giro stage win and top-5 after five-year victory drought and 2022 Tirreno win suggest genuine resurgence, though Dauphine 2022 hesitation shows inconsistency remains
